reviews.review_msg: Readings are insanely long for a first-year course, multiple movies per week, and so many lecture videos. The quizzes are computer-graded. I got a question marked as wrong because one letter wasn't capitalized. If you don't remember the exact wording he used in lectures, you'll get questions wrong on the tests.

Very course heavy for a first year course. Every week was a lot of reading, an oddly specific quiz where you had to remember everything, 2 hours of lectures, then another quiz on his lectures and the movies. The projects were peer graded and the quizzes were buggy and you had to email him to fix it. It was not enjoyable, unfortunately
Overall, if you are willing to keep up and absorb material to a tea, take it, otherwise skip out

Prof assigns 2 quizzes per week, graded by a computer, all answers are case sensitive. Divides his lectures into 7 - 10 different videos, plus 1 - 2 movies per week. Assignments peer graded. Quizzes are unfair and feel like trivia for movie buffs. Work load is way too heavy for an elective. Do not take this course.

By far the worst prof I've ever had. This person is not even a prof, careless and not professional. Made the course harder for no reason. As an elective for fun he made us to 30 quizzes. Literally made my grade 2 letter grades down because he didn't let me do the missed quiz due to illness.

The laziest professor I have ever had. I would have to email him at LEAST once a week because he uses an autograder that only accepts exact punctuation/capitalization, etc. He would also straight up put incorrect answers as the answer (wish I was joking) The two main assignments are peer graded so he literally doesn't mark a single thing.

Workload was heavy for a first year intro course, definitely not what most people are expecting from a cinema class, I would imagine. Quizes are auto-graded so if you forget to capitalize or hyphenate an answer, you get it wrong. You can email him to fix this but doing so on a weekly basis becomes tiresome. Essay grades are from peer reviews.

Course is basic intro to cinema. Workload with weekly quizzes and assigned reading's plus lecture and movie viewing felt heavy, and was hard to determine which material was tested for quizzes. Some questions did not feel very fair. Project worth 40% is interesting and fun to create, my favorite part of this course. All work is peer reviewed.
He doesn't actually do any grading your whole grade is based off of peer review scores...lowest effort prof ever. Way too much work for an elective, actually insane dont take it
Worst prof ever!!! Never take his classes!!

Peter is awesome! He is probably my favorite prof I've had in my four years of university. Class had no exam but was instead based on a series of group and individual projects which were a lot of work but he was very helpful along the way. Quick at replying to emails is always a plus. Also, he does a good mix of seminar and lecture/does both well.

Peter is hands down one of the best profs I have ever had. His lectures are amazing; he is engaging and hilarious, and he is so knowledgeable about the course contents. The course materials were interesting and diverse, and the workload was reasonable and incredibly interesting. I loved this class so much!
Peter was probably the best first year professor I have ever had. I would highly recommend taking a first year course with him.

Peter is a funny, caring prof. Lecture aren't mandatory to show up to but show up because there are performances sometimes. There's a lot of reading, and it's difficult to understand the purpose/point sometimes (go to tutorial!) Grading is fair, grading criteria is clear, overall an easy course.
Really great prof!  Has interesting lectures that really make you think!  He's kinda quirky and really funny in lectures, I recommend any English class he's teaching.
